title: Listening - Part 3: Changing a Room
description: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a change they made to a room at home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for making the change. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.
instructions: You are going to hear five extracts in which people talk about a change they made to a room at home. For questions 19–23, choose from the list A–H the main reason each speaker gives for making the change. Use the letters once only. There are three letters that you do not need to use.

extracts.context: Five people talk about changing a room in their home.
extracts.audio_script: Speaker 1 Woman: When I started working from home, I used my bedroom desk, but it was squeezed between the bed and a wardrobe and I couldn't spread papers out. I often ended up working at the kitchen table, where everyone interrupted me. [19]I cleared a corner of the spare room and put in a larger desk so I could concentrate on my job and keep my work materials organised in one place.[/19] It has made the distinction between working time and relaxing time much clearer too.||Speaker 2 Man: We recently adopted two rabbits. At first their indoor enclosure was in the living room, but it took up so much floor space that people constantly had to step around it. Our unused utility room had been full of old boxes. [20]We sorted everything out and turned that room into a safe area with enough floor space for the rabbits to move around freely.[/20] We still bring them into the living room sometimes, but they now have a permanent home of their own.||Speaker 3 Woman: My kitchen is quite small, and for years I kept pans stacked inside one another and ingredients in bags on the work surface. Whenever I cooked, finding anything involved moving several other things first. [21]I installed shelves and hooks because I needed proper places for equipment and food instead of having everything piled up in the limited space.[/21] It didn't increase the size of the kitchen, obviously, but now I can prepare a meal without first clearing the entire room.||Speaker 4 Man: Our friends often stay overnight after visiting, but the sofa in our spare room was old and very uncomfortable. People said they didn't mind, although I could see they slept badly. [22]We replaced it with a proper sofa bed and added a lamp and small table, because I wanted anyone staying at our house to feel relaxed and get a decent night's sleep.[/22] The room is still simple, but friends now volunteer to stay rather than trying to arrange a late journey home.||Speaker 5 Woman: I practise guitar most evenings, and my family never complained, but I knew that hearing the same difficult section repeated again and again couldn't be enjoyable. My bedroom wall is right next to my younger brother's. [23]I moved my music equipment into the room at the far end of the house and added thick curtains so that my practice would be less audible to everyone else.[/23] I feel less guilty now, and strangely I practise more because I’m not constantly wondering whether I'm annoying somebody.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_1
dialogue.text: When I started working from home, I used my bedroom desk, but it was squeezed between the bed and a wardrobe and I couldn't spread papers out. I often ended up working at the kitchen table, where everyone interrupted me. I cleared a corner of the spare room and put in a larger desk so I could concentrate on my job and keep my work materials organised in one place. It has made the distinction between working time and relaxing time much clearer too.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_2
dialogue.text: We recently adopted two rabbits. At first their indoor enclosure was in the living room, but it took up so much floor space that people constantly had to step around it. Our unused utility room had been full of old boxes. We sorted everything out and turned that room into a safe area with enough floor space for the rabbits to move around freely. We still bring them into the living room sometimes, but they now have a permanent home of their own.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_3
dialogue.text: My kitchen is quite small, and for years I kept pans stacked inside one another and ingredients in bags on the work surface. Whenever I cooked, finding anything involved moving several other things first. I installed shelves and hooks because I needed proper places for equipment and food instead of having everything piled up in the limited space. It didn't increase the size of the kitchen, obviously, but now I can prepare a meal without first clearing the entire room.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_4
dialogue.text: Our friends often stay overnight after visiting, but the sofa in our spare room was old and very uncomfortable. People said they didn't mind, although I could see they slept badly. We replaced it with a proper sofa bed and added a lamp and small table, because I wanted anyone staying at our house to feel relaxed and get a decent night's sleep. The room is still simple, but friends now volunteer to stay rather than trying to arrange a late journey home.

dialogue.speaker: speaker_5
dialogue.text: I practise guitar most evenings, and my family never complained, but I knew that hearing the same difficult section repeated again and again couldn't be enjoyable. My bedroom wall is right next to my younger brother's. I moved my music equipment into the room at the far end of the house and added thick curtains so that my practice would be less audible to everyone else. I feel less guilty now, and strangely I practise more because I’m not constantly wondering whether I'm annoying somebody.

options.text: to make the room appear brighter
to provide space for a pet

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: The change provides a suitable workspace that improves the speaker's ability to work (evidence [19]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: The room was reorganised to give the household's rabbits a safe and spacious area (evidence [20]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Shelves and hooks were added to improve storage in a small kitchen (evidence [21]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: The speaker changed the spare room to make overnight guests comfortable (evidence [22]).

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: The speaker relocated music practice so it would disturb family members less (evidence [23]).
